who highly recommended us, it still took all those touches before that guy was ready to buy. People give up far too early on their leads, on their clients, and even their clients for what they want to buy. I always think of the dentist. They come in, they look in your mouth, they say, hey, you need this kind of work done. It's going to be $10,000, well maybe you don't have $10,000 right then and there. But they don't bring it up until six months or a year later when you're back in front of them. And it's crazy. A lot of people, they have to think about decisions before they make purchases. They don't just instantly buy. So one of the things that you're a big proponent of, and we are too at GKIC, is sending out a print newsletter to your customers and to your leads. Why print? Why not just email? Hey man, email is free. Yeah, so that's a great question. Here's the thing that we found. The email newsletters simply don't work. We all have cluttered inboxes. Like most people, most people what they do when they first get up in the morning or when they're first going through their email in the afternoon or whatever period they go through and they look for as many emails to delete as they possibly can. So they go in and they click, click, click, click and hit delete. So now your newsletter just doesn't even get seen. So that's a problem. That's one problem. We all know deliverability rate is a problem with emails as well. If you send out a thousand emails, a thousand emails are not hitting the inbox. It's somewhere between about 30% and 70% of those emails will actually hit the inbox. Depending on their service provider, it's either just not being delivered, it's going into spam, it's going into the promotional category tab if they're using Google. So you've got challenges with deliverability. You also have everything else that's going on on the computer. So they go, even if they want to read your newsletter, they go to pull up your newsletter and they go to read it. And what happens? Someone instant messages them. Facebook beeps in. They see an ad that they want to click on. Whatever the case may be and they're gone, never to return to your email again, no matter what the good intention happens to be. And so what we've found is that you do not get the same response. People don't feel the same way about email newsletters that they do about a print newsletter. A study that, you know, so a guy I got this information from, his name is Jay Geyer, who he consults with Dan and he's in the dental niche. And he quoted a study at a recent event that I attended that he was putting on that the average newsletter, print newsletter that is read lingers around the house for up to four months. That is a long time to have your branding, your media sitting in the counter, moving its way into the office, making its way into the bathroom, you know, wherever it happens to go throughout their daily lives. That's a long time to have it sitting there and for your branding to be soaking in. Plus it's a lot easier to pass around a print. Well, I shouldn't say easier. It's easier to email out, you know, just forward an email, but people think it's spam when that happens, right? But when you walk over across the street to your neighbor and you go, you know, hey, Susie, you know, I just was reading this article and I thought about you and you hand them that newsletter. That is a referral right there. They are at least at a minimum endorsing you as a service provider. So, you know, just there's a lot more that goes into it. It gets a lot more attention. There's less competition in the mailbox. I was just working on an article the other day where saying that I love it when people say direct mail is dead. Because let them keep thinking it. Let them go their way. And I'm going to continue to make a lot of money on direct mail and have less competition. And so, you know, yeah, I mean, there's a lot of different reasons. Those are a few of the highlights, but I can tell you we send a lot of print newsletters for a lot of very smart companies. Some of them, 50, $60 million dollar companies, they're not sending out print newsletters without tracking it, without making sure it works. And for good quality leads, for their A leads or their A clients, it just doesn't make sense to do the email newsletter because they don't get open and they don't get read. Now, as far as referrals go, how does the newsletter that's sent in the mail
